message:
refactor(reports): route generate_session_report_with_cx through cx-first storage methods (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Complete the strangler-fig step for `generate_session_report_with_cx`:
route its 5 internal storage calls through the cx-first siblings
landed in ticks 115-120.

Before this tick, the cx-first session report had checkpoint seams
between sections but its internal storage calls still used the
legacy methods — meaning caller cancellation only fired at the
explicit `cx.checkpoint()` boundaries. After this tick, cancellation
propagates all the way down into the storage layer's pre-flight
checkpoints.

Migrated calls:
- `storage.get_events(...)` → `get_events_with_cx(cx, ...)`
- `storage.export_workflows(...)` → `export_workflows_with_cx(cx, ...)`
- `storage.get_step_logs(...)` → `get_step_logs_with_cx(cx, ...)`
- `storage.export_gaps(...)` → `export_gaps_with_cx(cx, ...)`
- `storage.get_audit_actions(...)` → `get_audit_actions_with_cx(cx, ...)`

Legacy `generate_session_report` preserved unchanged — it continues
to use the legacy storage methods.

Existing regression test `report_empty_db_with_cx_matches_legacy`
(from tick 88) pins the output-byte-equivalence contract: the
cx-first path must produce identical Markdown to the legacy path on
an uncancelled cx.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 47/47
reports tests pass, including the legacy-parity test.

First caller-migration tick in the storage-consumer strangler-fig
phase. Downstream files with `_with_cx` entry points can now start
routing their internal storage reads/writes through the cx-first
siblings, deepening cancellation propagation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
